name: Monterey Bay Aquarium
highlights: One of the world's great marine science institutions — opened 1984 on the site of a historic sardine cannery
Living Kelp Forest exhibit: a three-story tank of the actual ecosystem you swim above in Monterey Bay
Sea Otter Program rehabilitates and releases wild otters — often visible in the Great Tide Pool
Seafood Watch app helps travelers make sustainable seafood choices at restaurants everywhere

name: Henry Miller Memorial Library
highlights: A clearing in the redwoods — bookshop, gallery, and community gathering place
Regular readings, concerts, and film screenings in the outdoor amphitheater
Curated selection of Miller's work and books about the Big Sur experience
The cultural soul of Big Sur, maintained by volunteers since 1981

name: Robinson Jeffers' Tor House
highlights: Stone cottage and Hawk Tower built entirely by hand by poet Robinson Jeffers, 1914–1963
Jeffers carried each stone up from Carmel Beach and set them himself — the house is a poem
Saturday tours only; small groups; advance reservation required
Jeffers's poetry defined the Big Sur coast as sacred terrain — this is the source
admission: paid — reserve through Tor House Foundation

name: Carmel Mission Basilica
highlights: Founded in 1771 by Father Junípero Serra — the most historically significant site in the orbit
Serra is buried here; the mission was the headquarters of the California mission system
Museum with original artifacts; the basilica itself is architecturally stunning
Rose garden and courtyard offer quiet even during busy periods

The mission system displaced the Rumsien Ohlone people whose ancestral territory this is. The Rumsen Ohlone Tribal Community maintains a living presence in the region — this is contested ground, and an honest visit holds both the architecture and the history.

name: Hawthorne Gallery
location: Highway 1, Big Sur — directly across from Nepenthe
highlights: Multi-generational Big Sur family gallery — the Hawthornes have been part of the Big Sur art colony for decades
Painting, sculpture, and blown glass by artists who live and work on this coast. The landscape is in the work.
Free to visit, genuinely browsable, no pressure. The kind of gallery where you learn something about a place by looking at how its artists see it.
Across the road from Nepenthe — pair with lunch and the terrace view for a full Big Sur afternoon

name: California Condor Viewing — Ventana Wildlife Society
location: Big Sur coast — viewing from Highway 1 pullouts, especially near Anderson Creek and Pfeiffer Big Sur
highlights: California condors — 9.5-foot wingspan, once down to 22 birds on Earth — soar the Big Sur thermals thanks to Ventana Wildlife Society's recovery program
The condors are wild and free-flying. They're tagged with numbered wing tags — you can look up individual birds' histories on the Ventana WS website.
Best viewing: Highway 1 pullouts between Pfeiffer Big Sur and Julia Pfeiffer Burns, especially on warm afternoons when thermals are strongest
Ventana Wildlife Society has been releasing captive-bred condors into the Big Sur wild since 1997. This is conservation as lived practice, not a zoo exhibit.
bookingWindow: Self-guided — free, no reservation. Check ventanaws.org for condor sighting reports and educational programs.
hours: Daylight hours — warm afternoons best for thermals
